vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on motor mount due to cooling airflow.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal analysis and adjust cooling inlet design if necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure at spar attachment flange under dynamic loads.
risk.mit: Perform structural analysis and reinforce if needed.

risk.desc: Insufficient access for maintenance of equipment bay door hinge.
risk.mit: Increase size of service access cutout to ensure easy access.

risk.desc: Process limitations may affect surface finish of cooling inlet.

risk.desc: Tolerance mismatch between motor mount and adjacent motor pylon.
risk.mit: Review and adjust tolerances to ensure proper fit.

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structural integrity under lift and landing loads
Continuous load path from wing skin to fuselage frames
Cooling airflow from battery bay to payload bay
Control motion transmission from flight control surfaces to wing tips
Electrical power and data transmission from avionics hub to sensor modules
Anti-rotation mechanism for main spar
Service access for battery bay and payload bay
Datum visibility for alignment and assembly

verify.rule: All required evidence_features are present in part_tree/geometry_ops.
Mass estimate matches specified requirements.
Each physical feature in geometry_ops can be traced back to its corresponding entry in part_tree.
Adjacent interfaces are reciprocal and consistent between neighboring subsystems.
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interface geometries.
risk.mit: Implement additional alignment aids and visual indicators.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion affecting structural integrity over long missions.
risk.mit: Use thermally stable materials and incorporate expansion joints.

risk.desc: Cooling channel may become obstructed if not properly routed.
risk.mit: Ensure unobstructed airflow and proper routing during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of improper preload on fasteners leading to loosening over time.
risk.mit: Specify torque values and conduct regular maintenance checks.

risk.desc: Potential for misidentification of harness interfaces during installation.
risk.mit: Label all harness interfaces clearly and provide installation instructions.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Lift and landing loads transferred from wing skin to fuselage frames via spars
Cooling airflow from battery bay to payload bay through spar channels
Thermal management from sun exposure to wing structure via heat sinks
Aileron control motion transmitted from flight control surfaces to wing tips via spars
Electrical power and data transmission from avionics hub to sensor modules through spar harnesses

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection and CMM for critical dimensions

verification_focus.check_item: alignment and fit with fuselage frame
